markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' official delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, has extended the window for events to unfold before launch, shaping trader focus on major cultural and global milestones. After shifting from an earlier May 2026 target announced in 2025, the studio cited additional time needed for polish on the Vice City-set open world. This timeline fuels speculation around high-profile releases, music drops, and geopolitical shifts that could resolve before the highly anticipated debut. With GTA Online updates continuing as a bridge and industry attention centered on Rockstar's quality standards, the market reflects bets on verifiable developments in entertainment and news that align with historical patterns of long development cycles for blockbuster titles.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape trial on May 15, 2026, has sharply elevated trader expectations for no additional prison time, currently implied at 75.9%. With one prior 2025 conviction carrying up to 25 years and a consecutive 16-year California sentence already in place, the hung jury on the Jessica Mann charge removed immediate momentum for further convictions. Weinstein’s advanced age, documented health complications including cancer and diabetes, and ongoing plea discussions for unresolved counts introduce substantial uncertainty around sentencing timelines and potential reductions. Historical patterns in high-profile cases show health and procedural factors often influence outcomes more than initial verdicts, supporting the market’s current pricing of limited incremental incarceration risk.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold the strongest trader consensus at 56.5% due to their league-leading 53-22-7 regular-season record and current 2-1 Eastern Conference Final series lead over the Montreal Canadiens. Vegas Golden Knights sit at 42.6% after sweeping the Presidents’ Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche to claim the Western Conference title, showcasing veteran depth and resilience on a grueling playoff path. Montreal trails at 1.5% as the lower-seeded Atlantic Division team that advanced through extended series but now faces a deficit against Carolina’s defensive structure and home-ice advantage in the conference final. These standings and recent playoff results reflect the market’s assessment of roster health, historical postseason experience, and matchup implications heading into the Stanley Cup Final.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Montreal Canadiens win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold the strongest trader consensus at 56.5% due to their league-leading 53-22-7 regular-season record and current 2-1 Eastern Conference Final series lead over the Montreal Canadiens. Vegas Golden Knights sit at 42.6% after sweeping the Presidents’ Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche to claim the Western Conference title, showcasing veteran depth and resilience on a grueling playoff path. Montreal trails at 1.5% as the lower-seeded Atlantic Division team that advanced through extended series but now faces a deficit against Carolina’s defensive structure and home-ice advantage in the conference final. These standings and recent playoff results reflect the market’s assessment of roster health, historical postseason experience, and matchup implications heading into the Stanley Cup Final.

markets.question: Will the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Oklahoma City Thunder win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder lead the 2026 NBA Champion market at 43.5% due to their league-best 64-18 regular-season record, elite defense anchored by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, and proven depth that carried them through early playoff sweeps. The New York Knicks sit at 31.4% after sweeping the Cleveland Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference finals, powered by Jalen Brunson’s scoring and Karl-Anthony Towns’ interior presence while eyeing home-court advantage in a potential Finals matchup. San Antonio Spurs hold 27.3% on the strength of Victor Wembanyama’s historic two-way play and a 62-20 campaign bolstered by De’Aaron Fox, though their Western Conference finals deficit against Oklahoma City has kept their implied probability in check amid recent series swings. Trader consensus reflects these teams’ regular-season form, roster construction, and current playoff positioning heading into the conference finals.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit as narrow co-favorites in the 2026 World Cup winner market, with England, Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina closely bunched behind, reflecting evenly matched squads across Europe and South America. Spain’s blend of youth and experience, highlighted by Lamine Yamal’s potential return from a hamstring issue, underpins their position alongside France’s proven depth and recent final appearances. England’s consistent tournament pedigree, combined with strong qualifying form from Portugal and the defending champion Argentina, sustains the tight spread. The expanded 48-team field and favorable group draws for several contenders further compress probabilities, as traders price in multiple viable paths through the knockout stages starting June 11.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s entrenched control as General Secretary of the Communist Party and Chairman of the Central Military Commission underpins trader expectations that he will remain in power through the end of 2026. Ongoing anti-corruption purges, including early 2026 military leadership changes that removed senior figures without triggering visible elite resistance, have further centralized authority while leaving no designated successor. Active high-level diplomatic engagements across multiple regions and his New Year address emphasizing policy continuity on priorities such as Taiwan have reinforced perceptions of stability. Analysts highlight the absence of confirmed health issues or institutional challenges before the 21st Party Congress in late 2027, where leadership adjustments are widely anticipated to extend his role rather than end it.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential primary features Gavin Newsom as the early market leader at 23.9 percent, reflecting his executive record as California governor, term-limited status, and sustained national profile through public exchanges with the Trump administration. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 10 percent draws support from progressive voters and younger demographics, while Kamala Harris at 8 percent retains name recognition from her prior nomination. Recent national polls show fluctuating support among Newsom, Pete Buttigieg, and Harris, underscoring how endorsements, fundraising, and issue positioning on the economy and immigration could consolidate backing ahead of formal announcements and primaries. Trader consensus prices this fragmented field as highly uncertain more than two years before voting begins.
